india_trends.title: Singapore Airlines says Pakistan's airspace closure hurt Air India more than foreign airlines
india_trends.summary: Singapore Airlines CEO Goh Choon Phong highlighted that Air India's inability to use Pakistani airspace puts it at a disadvantage compared to foreign carriers. The closure has impacted Air India's operational costs and contributed to its mounting losses amidst broader industry challenges.

india_trends.summary: Delhi Metro Rail Corporation has introduced a new hydrogen-powered shuttle bus service in Central Vista. Indian Oil Corporation Limited is providing two advanced buses for this initiative. The service aims to offer eco-friendly last-mile connectivity. It will operate on working days during peak offi

india_trends.title: Max Healthcare sets board meeting date to declare Q4 results 2026, final dividend for FY26
india_trends.summary: Max Healthcare Institute's Board of Directors will meet on May 21, 2026, to review financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026, and discuss a potential final dividend. The trading window for securities will remain closed until 48 hours after results are declared.

india_trends.title: Rapido raises $240 million led by Prosus at $3 billion valuation
india_trends.summary: Rapido will use the fresh capital to deepen expansion beyond metros, strengthen supply and invest in technology and drivers.

india_trends.title: Flipkart IPO likely to be delayed as Walmart-owned online retailer to prioritise profitability, says report
india_trends.summary: Walmart has advised Flipkart to delay its IPO plans and prioritize achieving EBITDA breakeven by FY27. This decision, made during Walmart CEO John Furner's visit to India, aims for long-term profitability, marking a significant delay in Walmart's listings in the country.

india_trends.title: UK bond yields leap as Burnham sees possible route to power
india_trends.summary: Borrowing costs ​for governments around the world were ⁠rising on Friday ‌on renewed worries about the inflationary impact ​of the Iran ​war.

india_trends.title: Rupee falls 7% year-to-date, and it may fall more this year; here's why
india_trends.summary: The Indian rupee has fallen nearly 7% this year due to soaring crude oil prices and foreign capital outflows. The rupee's weakness is likely to continue- some experts believe the domestic unit may touch the 100 mark if oil prices continue rising.

india_trends.title: Explained: What is US Senate’s CLARITY Act and why does it matter for crypto investors?
india_trends.summary: The Republican-led Senate Banking Committee has advanced the long-awaited CLARITY Act, a landmark crypto regulation bill aimed at defining digital assets, resolving SEC–CFTC jurisdiction overlap, and setting rules for decentralised platforms. The move brings long-needed regulatory clarity, boosting

india_trends.title: Japanese bond yields hit record highs as rate-hike bets firm
india_trends.summary: The benchmark 10-year JGB yield rose as much as 10 basis points (bps) to 2.73%, the highest ‌level since ⁠May 1997. The ⁠yields on five-year notes and 20-year bonds touched all-time peaks of 2.00% ​and 3.615%, respectively. Yields move inversely to bond prices.

india_trends.title: PM Modi in UAE: India signs defence, gas supply and strategic oil reserves pacts with UAE
india_trends.summary: India and the United Arab Emirates forged new ties on Friday. Key agreements were signed in defence cooperation, energy security, and infrastructure investment. The UAE committed $5 billion to Indian infrastructure and financial institutions. These pacts were finalized during Prime Minister Narendra

india_trends.title: Global Markets | Japan's Nikkei slumps to weekly loss on tech share profit-taking
india_trends.summary: Chip-testing equipment maker Advantest was ​the biggest drag, slumping 7.9% to shave 544 index points from the Nikkei. ​Fellow heavyweight Tokyo Electron lost 1.8%.

india_trends.title: Australian shares flat as housing-tax changes, global risks cap volatile week
india_trends.summary: Australia's federal budget on Tuesday ​proposed curbing negative gearing to encourage investment in new housing, raising concerns about slower mortgage demand.

india_trends.title: Bitcoin slips back to $81K after rallying to $82K on CLARITY Act optimism
india_trends.summary: Bitcoin briefly surged past $82,000 following positive developments from The CLARITY Act, boosting investor sentiment. Despite a slight pullback to $81,000, analysts suggest regulatory progress and strong equity markets could attract institutional capital, potentially pushing Bitcoin towards $85,000
